lines.lyrics: Her green plastic watering can  For her fake Chinese rubber plant
In the fake plastic earth  That she bought from a rubber man
In a town full of rubber plans  To get rid of itself  It wears her out,
it wears her out  It wears her out, it wears her out
She lives with a broken man  A cracked polystyrene man
Who just crumbles and burns  He used to do surgery
For girls in the eighties  But gravity always wins  It wears him out,
it wears her out  It wears her out, it wears her out
She looks like the real thing  She tastes like the real thing
My fake plastic love  But I can't help the feeling
I could blow through the ceiling  If I just turn and run
it wears me out  It wears me out, it wears me out
If I could be who you wanted
If I could be who you wanted all  the time  All the time...
